title: Top 10 Fully Funded Scholarships for Indian Students in 2025
description: Complete list of best fully funded scholarships available for Indian students to study in India and abroad in 2025.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
India has a large number of scholarship opportunities for deserving students. Here are the top 10 fully funded scholarships available in 2025.

**1. Prime Minister Research Fellowship (PMRF)**
This flagship fellowship offers PhD students up to Rs.80,000 per month stipend along with a research grant. It is one of the most prestigious scholarships in India for science and engineering graduates.

**2. INSPIRE Scholarship by DST**
The Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research scholarship supports students pursuing natural sciences at undergraduate and postgraduate level. It provides Rs.80,000 per year.

**3. Fulbright-Nehru Fellowship**
For Indian students who want to pursue Master's or research programs in the USA, the Fulbright-Nehru fellowship is a golden opportunity. It covers full tuition, living expenses and travel.

**4. Chevening Scholarship**
The UK government's Chevening Scholarship is available for outstanding Indian students. It covers full tuition at any UK university plus living allowance.

**5. Commonwealth Scholarship**
The Commonwealth Scholarship Commission offers fully funded scholarships for Master's and PhD students from Commonwealth countries including India.

**6. DAAD Scholarship Germany**
Germany offers some of the best scholarships for Indian students through DAAD. These cover Masters and PhD programs at German universities.

**7. NSP Central Sector Scholarship**
The National Scholarship Portal offers central sector scholarships to meritorious students from economically weaker backgrounds.

**8. Maulana Azad Fellowship**
For students from minority communities pursuing MPhil and PhD, the MANF provides monthly stipend and contingency grant.

**9. Telangana ePass Scholarship**
Telangana government provides full fee reimbursement for SC, ST, BC and EBC students studying in recognized colleges.

**10. AICTE Pragati Scholarship**
For girl students in technical education, AICTE Pragati provides Rs.50,000 per year for tuition and other expenses.

faqs.q: Who can apply for fully funded scholarships in India?
faqs.a: Students from various categories including merit students, SC/ST/OBC students, minority students, and students from economically weaker sections can apply based on eligibility.
Aadhaar card, caste certificate if applicable, income certificate, mark sheets, bank account details, and admission proof are commonly required.

faqs.q: Is there an age limit for scholarships?
faqs.a: Age limit varies by scholarship. Most government scholarships do not have a strict age limit but are tied to the course duration.

title: How to Apply for NSP Scholarship 2025 Step by Step
description: Complete step-by-step guide to apply for National Scholarship Portal scholarships for Indian students.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
The National Scholarship Portal (NSP) is the single window platform for all government scholarships in India. Here is how to apply.

**Step 1: Register on NSP**
Visit scholarships.gov.in and click on New Registration. Fill in your basic details including name, date of birth, mobile number and email ID.

**Step 2: Verify Mobile Number**
An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP to verify and complete registration.

**Step 3: Login and Fill Application**
Login with your Application ID and password. Select the appropriate scholarship scheme and fill in all required details.

**Step 4: Upload Documents**
Upload scanned copies of Aadhaar card, income certificate, caste certificate, bank passbook and mark sheets in the required format.

**Step 5: Submit Application**
Verify all details and submit the application. Note down your application number for future reference.

**Step 6: Institute Verification**
Your college or institute will verify your application on the NSP portal. Ensure you follow up with your institution.

**Step 7: District Verification**
After institute verification, the application goes to the district welfare office for final verification.

**Step 8: Track Status**
You can track your application status on the NSP portal using your application ID.

faqs.a: NSP is the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in which is a one-stop platform for all government scholarship schemes.

faqs.q: Can I apply for multiple scholarships on NSP?
faqs.a: Generally you can apply for one scholarship under each category but cannot receive multiple scholarships for the same purpose.

faqs.q: What if my application is rejected?
faqs.a: You can check the rejection reason on the portal and reapply in the next cycle after rectifying the issue.

title: Telangana ePass Scholarship 2025 Complete Guide
description: Everything you need to know about Telangana ePass scholarship including eligibility, documents, and how to apply.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
Telangana ePass is the state government scholarship portal for students belonging to SC, ST, BC, EBC and other categories in Telangana.

**What is ePass?**
ePass stands for Electronic Payment and Application System for Scholarships. It is the Telangana government portal through which eligible students receive fee reimbursement and post-matric scholarships.

**Categories Covered**
- Scheduled Castes (SC)
- Scheduled Tribes (ST)
- Backward Classes (BC)
- Economically Backward Classes (EBC)
- Disabled Students
- Muslims and other minorities under specific schemes

**Benefits**
The scholarship covers full tuition fee reimbursement for eligible students. Hostellers also receive maintenance allowance.

**Eligibility**
Students must be domiciled in Telangana and studying in recognized colleges. Family income limits apply as per the category.

**Documents Required**
- Caste certificate issued by competent authority
- Income certificate
- Aadhaar card
- Bank passbook (student's name)
- College bonafide certificate
- Previous year mark sheet
- Residence proof

**How to Apply**
Applications are submitted through your college. The college data entry operator fills in your details on the ePass portal. Ensure all documents are submitted to your college in time.

**Important Dates**
Applications usually open in August and close by November each year. Check the official portal for exact dates.

faqs.q: Who is eligible for Telangana ePass scholarship?
faqs.a: SC, ST, BC, EBC and disabled students domiciled in Telangana studying in recognized institutions are eligible.
The scholarship amount is directly credited to the student bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

faqs.q: What if my college is not in Telangana?
faqs.a: Students studying outside Telangana may still be eligible. Contact the district welfare office for guidance.

title: Study in Germany Without IELTS for Indian Students 2025
description: How Indian students can study in Germany at top universities without IELTS requirement in 2025.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
Germany is one of the most popular study abroad destinations for Indian students. Many programs do not require IELTS if taught in English.

**Why Study in Germany?**
Germany offers tuition-free education at public universities. Indian students need to pay only a semester contribution fee of around 150 to 350 euros per semester.

**Programs Without IELTS**
Many English-taught Master's programs in Germany accept Duolingo English Test, TOEFL, or proof of English medium education from recognized universities.

**Top German Universities**
- Technical University of Munich
- Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
- Heidelberg University
- RWTH Aachen University
- University of Stuttgart

**DAAD Scholarship**
The DAAD scholarship is the best option for Indian students. It provides monthly stipend, travel allowance and health insurance.

**Application Process**
1. Choose a program at a German university
2. Check the language requirement
3. Prepare your documents
4. Apply through the university portal
5. Apply for DAAD scholarship if eligible
6. Get admission letter
7. Apply for German student visa

**Blocked Account Requirement**
To get a German student visa, Indian students need to open a blocked account and deposit approximately 11,208 euros (as per 2025 requirement) which is released monthly during your stay.

faqs.q: Do I need IELTS to study in Germany?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Many programs accept Duolingo, TOEFL or English medium certificate from your previous institution.
Public universities in Germany charge only a semester contribution fee of 150-350 euros. There is no tuition fee.

faqs.q: What is a blocked account for Germany?
faqs.a: It is a special bank account in which you deposit approximately 11,208 euros before applying for a German student visa. The amount is released at a fixed rate each month.

title: Scholarships for Girls in India 2025 Complete List
description: Complete list of government and private scholarships exclusively available for girl students in India in 2025.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
India has many scholarship programs exclusively for girl students across different levels of education.

**1. AICTE Pragati Scholarship**
For girl students in technical education. Provides Rs.50,000 per year for engineering and polytechnic students.

**2. CBSE Merit Scholarship for Single Girl Child**
For single girl child who has passed Class 10 from CBSE schools with 60 percent or above marks.

**3. Indira Gandhi Scholarship for Single Girl Child**
UGC provides this scholarship for postgraduate students who are the only girl child in the family.

**4. Women Scientist Scheme (WOS-A)**
Department of Science and Technology provides this for women scientists who have taken a career break.

**5. Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship**
For minority girl students from Class 9 to 12 with family income below Rs.2 lakh per year.

**6. Mukesh Patel Scholarship for Girls**
For girl students in STEM fields at undergraduate level from economically weaker sections.

**7. State Government Scholarships for Girls**
Almost every state government has special scholarship schemes for girl students. Check your state welfare portal.

**How to Apply**
Most girl scholarships are available on the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in. Some are available directly on the respective organization websites.

faqs.q: Are there special scholarships only for girls in India?
faqs.a: Yes, AICTE Pragati, CBSE single girl child scholarship, Indira Gandhi scholarship and many state schemes are exclusively for girl students.

faqs.q: Can married women apply for scholarships?
faqs.a: Yes, many scholarships do not have a restriction on marital status. WOS-A specifically targets women scientists with career breaks.

faqs.q: Where can I find all girl scholarships in one place?
faqs.a: The National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in lists all central government scholarships for girl students.

title: Fellowships for PhD Students in India 2025
description: Best research fellowships available for PhD students in India with stipend details and how to apply.
author: ScholarBuddy Team
content:
PhD research in India is supported by several prestigious fellowship programs that provide monthly stipend and research grants.

**1. CSIR-JRF (Junior Research Fellowship)**
Provided by Council of Scientific and Industrial Research. Stipend is Rs.37,000 per month for JRF and Rs.42,000 for SRF. Requires clearing CSIR-UGC NET.

**2. UGC-JRF**
University Grants Commission provides JRF to students who clear UGC NET with JRF qualification. Stipend is Rs.37,000 per month.

**3. ICMR Fellowship**
Indian Council of Medical Research provides fellowship for biomedical research. Stipend is Rs.37,000 per month.

**4. DBT Research Fellowship**
Department of Biotechnology provides fellowship for life science research students. Cleared GATE or BET required.

**5. PRIME MINISTER RESEARCH FELLOWSHIP (PMRF)**
PMRF is one of the highest stipend fellowships offering Rs.70,000 to Rs.80,000 per month for PhD at IITs and IISc.

**6. SERB National Post Doctoral Fellowship**
For PhD holders who want to continue research. Provides Rs.55,000 per month for 2 years.

**7. NBHM Scholarship**
National Board of Higher Mathematics provides fellowship for PhD students in mathematics. Stipend is Rs.25,000 per month.

**Which Fellowship to Choose?**
Choose based on your subject: CSIR-JRF for physical science, UGC-JRF for humanities and social science, ICMR for medical, DBT for biotechnology, PMRF for engineering.

faqs.q: What is the difference between JRF and SRF?
faqs.a: JRF is Junior Research Fellowship for the first 2 years. After completing 2 years with satisfactory progress, it upgrades to SRF (Senior Research Fellowship) with higher stipend.

faqs.q: Is NET mandatory for all fellowships?
faqs.a: Not for all. GATE is acceptable for engineering fellowships. PMRF has its own selection process. ICMR has BET exam.

faqs.q: Can I hold two fellowships at the same time?
faqs.a: No. You cannot hold two fellowships simultaneously. You must select one and inform the awarding agency.
